_instructions: Copy to specs/features/[feature-name].spec.md (or .json). Fill every field. Empty fields produce worse agent output than no spec at all. Delete this _instructions field before committing.
intent: [One sentence: what is being built and why. E.g.: Enable authenticated users to export their transaction history as a CSV file so they can reconcile against external records.]
acceptanceCriteria: [Observable outcome 1 — e.g.: Authenticated user can trigger a CSV export from the account dashboard]
[Observable outcome 2 — e.g.: Export covers any 90-day window selected by the user]
[Observable outcome 3 — e.g.: User receives an email with a download link when the export is ready]
[Add more as needed — each criterion maps to at least one test scenario]

architecture.notes: [Any architectural constraints, e.g.: Export must be async — no inline downloads. Max 90-day window to bound query cost.]

testScenarios.name: Happy path — export triggered and completed
testScenarios.given: [Initial state, e.g.: Authenticated user with 30 days of transaction data]
testScenarios.when: [Action, e.g.: User triggers export for the last 30 days]
testScenarios.then: [Expected outcome, e.g.: ExportJob created with status=queued, email sent when status=complete, CSV contains correct rows]

testScenarios.name: Window exceeds 90-day maximum
testScenarios.then: 400 response with clear error message, no ExportJob created

outOfScope: [e.g.: PDF export format — deferred to v2]
[e.g.: Custom column selection — deferred to v2]
[e.g.: Admin-triggered exports on behalf of users]

implementationPlan._note: Generated by writing-plans skill. Each task is one agent session. After the last task completes the harness enters integration phase — run 'bash scripts/workflow/mark-integration-passed.sh' to verify the full suite together before release.

tasks.doneWhen: [e.g.: Migration runs cleanly, schema matches dataShapes above]
